tool.name: 3DTrees: SmartTile
tool.description: Subsampling, tiling, merging and matching of (multiple) point clouds
tool.command:
## Create output directory mkdir -p output_dir &&
## Task-specific input setup and command execution #if $operation.task == 'tile': ## Create input directory and symlink files mkdir -p input_dir && #for $f in $operation.input_files: ln -s '$f' input_dir/'${f.element_identifier}.laz' && #end for
## Run tile task python -u /src/run.py --task tile --input-dir input_dir --output-dir output_dir --tile-length '$operation.tile_length' --tile-buffer '$operation.tile_buffer' #if $operation.tiling_threshold: --tiling-threshold '$operation.tiling_threshold' #end if --resolution-1 '$operation.resolution_1' --resolution-2 '$operation.resolution_2' #if $operation.skip_dimension_reduction: --skip-dimension-reduction True #else: --skip-dimension-reduction False #end if --chunk-size '$operation.chunk_size' --num-spatial-chunks \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} --workers \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} --threads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4}
#elif $operation.task == 'merge': ## Create input directories and symlink files mkdir -p input_segmented && #for $f in $operation.input_segmented: ln -s '$f' input_segmented/'${f.element_identifier}.laz' && #end for
mkdir -p input_res1 && #for $f in $operation.input_res1: ln -s '$f' input_res1/'${f.element_identifier}.laz' && #end for
#if $operation.input_original: mkdir -p input_original && #for $f in $operation.input_original: ln -s '$f' input_original/'${f.element_identifier}.laz' && #end for #end if
## Run merge task python -u /src/run.py --task merge --subsampled-segmented-folder input_segmented --subsampled-target-folder input_res1 --tile_bounds_json '$operation.tile_bounds_json' #if $operation.input_original: --original-input-dir input_original #end if --output-tiles-folder output_dir/output_tiles --output-merged-laz output_dir/merged.laz --buffer '$operation.buffer' --overlap-threshold '$operation.overlap_threshold' --max-centroid-distance '$operation.max_centroid_distance' --max-volume-for-merge '$operation.max_volume_for_merge' --min-cluster-size '$operation.min_cluster_size' --border-zone-width '$operation.border_zone_width' --instance-dimension '$operation.instance_dimension' $operation.disable_matching $operation.transfer_original_dims_to_merged --threedtrees-dims '$operation.threedtrees_dims' --threedtrees-suffix '$operation.threedtrees_suffix' --workers \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2}
#elif $operation.task == 'remap': ## Separate folders: originals with dims in output_originals, merged file in output_merged ln -s '$operation.merged_file' merged_input.laz && mkdir -p input_original output_originals output_merged && #for $f in $operation.original_files: ln -s '$f' input_original/'${f.element_identifier}.laz' && #end for python -u /src/run.py --task remap --merged-laz merged_input.laz --original-input-dir input_original --output-dir output_originals --workers \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2} $operation.transfer_original_dims_to_merged --threedtrees-dims '$operation.threedtrees_dims' --threedtrees-suffix '$operation.threedtrees_suffix' #if $operation.transfer_original_dims_to_merged: --output-merged-with-originals output_merged/merged_with_originals.laz #end if #end if

param.name: input_files
param.label: Input LAZ/LAS files
param.help: Collection of point cloud files to tile and subsample

param.label: Tile Length
param.help: Size of tiles in meters (default: 300m)

param.label: Tile Buffer
param.help: Overlap/buffer between tiles in meters (default: 20m)

param.label: Tiling Threshold (MB)
param.help: File size threshold in MB. If input folder has single file below this size, skip tiling (optional)

param.label: Resolution 1 (m)
param.help: First subsampling resolution in meters (default: 0.01 = 1cm)

param.label: Resolution 2 (m)
param.help: Second subsampling resolution in meters (default: 0.1 = 10cm)

param.label: Skip Dimension Reduction
param.help: Keep all point dimensions instead of reducing to XYZ-only. Uncheck for raw pre-segmentation data (default: reduce to XYZ).

param.label: Chunk size (points)
param.help: Points per chunk when reading LAZ/LAS in tiling phase 1. Smaller values reduce peak RAM and increase overhead (default: 20,000,000).

param.label: Workers
param.help: Number of workers for parallel processing (default: 2)

param.name: input_segmented
param.label: Segmented files (e.g.10cm)
param.help: Collection of subsampled LAZ files with predictions (PredInstance/PredSemantic dimensions)

param.name: input_res1
param.label: Subsampled target files (e.g. 1cm)
param.help: Collection of subsampled LAZ files at target resolution (for remapping)

param.name: input_original
param.label: Original input files (optional)
param.help: Optional collection of original input LAZ files for final remap to original resolution

param.name: tile_bounds_json
param.label: Tile bounds JSON (tile_bounds_tindex.json from tile task)
param.help: Grid definition from the Tile task (required). Merge uses the same tile grid as tiling for robust matching.

param.label: Buffer Distance (m)
param.help: Buffer distance for filtering in meters (default: 10.0m)

param.label: Overlap Threshold
param.help: Overlap ratio threshold for instance matching (0.3 = 30%)

param.label: Max Centroid Distance (m)
param.help: Maximum centroid distance to merge instances in meters (default: 3.0m)

param.label: Max Volume for Merge (m³)
param.help: Max convex hull volume for small instance merging in m³ (default: 4.0m³)

param.label: Minimum Cluster Size
param.help: Minimum cluster size in points for reassignment (default: 300)

param.label: Border Zone Width (m)
param.help: Width of border zone beyond buffer for instance matching in meters (default: 10.0m)

param.label: Disable Matching
param.help: Disable cross-tile instance matching (default: False)

param.label: Instance dimension
param.help: Name of the instance ID dimension in segmented files. Default: PredInstance (SegmentAnything-style). Use treeID for other segmentation tools.

param.label: Transfer original attributes to merged point cloud
param.help: Add dimensions from original files (e.g. Intensity, RGB) to the merged LAZ. When disabled, merged output keeps only merge dimensions. Applies to single-file merge path.

param.label: Added 3DT dimensions
param.help: Comma-separated list of dimension names produced by 3DTrees to transfer to original files. Renamed to 3DT_{name}_{suffix} format.

validator.message: Only letters and commas are allowed

param.label: 3DTrees suffix
param.help: Suffix for 3DTrees dimension branding (e.g. SAT → 3DT_PredInstance_SAT) for SegmentAnyTree.

validator.message: Only letters and commas are allowed

param.label: Workers
param.help: Number of workers for parallel processing (default: 2)

param.name: merged_file
param.label: Merged LAZ file
param.help: Single merged LAZ file with new dimensions (e.g. from Merge task). All dimensions from this file will be added to the original files by nearest-neighbor (no instance dimension required).

param.name: original_files
param.label: Original LAZ/LAS files
param.help: Original point cloud files. Output will have the same files with all dimensions from the merged file added.

param.label: Transfer original attributes to merged point cloud
param.help: Write a merged LAZ with dimensions from original files added (merged_with_originals.laz). When disabled, this output is skipped.

param.label: 3DTrees dimensions
param.help: Comma-separated list of dimension names produced by 3DTrees to transfer to original files. Renamed to 3DT_{name}_{suffix} format.

validator.message: Only letters and commas are allowed

param.label: 3DTrees suffix
param.help: Suffix for 3DTrees dimension branding (e.g. SAT → 3DT_PredInstance_SAT).

validator.message: Only letters and commas are allowed

collection.name: output_originals_with_dims
collection.label: ${tool.name}: Original files with dimensions from merged file

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool processes 3D point cloud data for tree segmentation with three task modes:
1. **Tile**: Subsample input point clouds and create overlapping tiles for processing 2. **Merge**: Remaps predictions to target resolution and merge tiles with instance matching (requires Tile bounds JSON from Tile task) 3. **Remap**: One merged LAZ file and the original files folder; outputs original files with **all dimensions from the merged file** added (no hardcoded dimension names)
**Tile Task**
Processes input LAZ/LAS files through tiling and subsampling (laspy + PDAL): - Creates overlapping tiles - Generates two subsampled resolutions (default: 1cm and 10cm)
*Parameters:* - Tile Length: Size of tiles in meters (default: 300m in form; tool default 100m) - Tile Buffer: Overlap between tiles in meters (default: 20m) - Tiling Threshold: File size threshold in MB - files below this skip tiling (optional) - Resolution 1: First subsampling resolution in meters (default: 0.01 = 1cm) - Resolution 2: Second subsampling resolution in meters (default: 0.1 = 10cm) - Skip Dimension Reduction: Keep all point dimensions; uncheck to reduce to XYZ only
*Outputs:* Collection of tiled and subsampled LAZ files organized in subdirectories
**Merge Task**
Complete merge workflow: 1. Remaps segmented (e.g. 10cm) predictions to target resolution (e.g. 1cm) 2. Merges overlapping tiles with cross-tile instance matching 3. Optionally remaps to original input resolution when original files are provided
**Tile bounds JSON is required** for the merge task (use the Tile bounds JSON output from the Tile task).
*Parameters:* - Instance dimension: Name of the instance ID dimension in segmented LAZ files (default: **PredInstance**; use **treeID** for other segmentation tools). Merge uses this for cross-tile matching and remap. - Tile bounds JSON: Required. Grid definition from the Tile task. - Input: Collection of 10cm subsampled LAZ files with predictions - Target: Subsampled files at target resolution (e.g. 2cm) - Buffer: Buffer distance for filtering in meters (default: 10.0m) - Overlap Threshold: Ratio for instance matching, 0-1 (default: 0.3 = 30%) - Max Centroid Distance: Max distance to merge instances (default: 3.0m) - Max Volume for Merge: Max volume for small instance merging (default: 4.0m³) - Min Cluster Size: Minimum points per cluster (default: 300) - Border Zone Width: Width of border zone for matching (default: 10.0m)
*Outputs:* - Collection of per-tile segmented LAZ files (when original input files are provided) - Single merged LAZ file
**Remap task**
Takes one merged LAZ file and the original files (folder/collection). Outputs: (1) original-resolution files with **all dimensions from the merged file** added; (2) a single merged LAZ file with **dimensions from the original files** added (e.g. Intensity, ReturnNumber, Classification), filled by nearest-neighbor matching. Dimension names are taken from the merged/original headers (no hardcoded dimension names).
*Inputs:* Merged LAZ file; Original LAZ/LAS files (multiple). *Outputs:* Collection of original files with dimensions from the merged file added; Merged LAZ with original dimensions.
**Processing Notes**
- All tasks use parallel processing with configurable workers (defaults to Galaxy job slots) - Tiling uses spatial chunks aligned to voxel grid to prevent duplicate points - Merge identifies whole trees, reassigns small clusters, and handles cross-tile instances - Remap uses cKDTree for efficient nearest neighbor queries

citations.citation: @misc{3dtrees_tile_merge, title = {3D Trees Tile and Merge Tool}, author = {3D Trees Project}, year = {2025}}
